summary: Iteration 4 — RETEST of the critical auto-detect-on-CSV-change bug from iteration_3. Verified that the debounced useEffect at NewAllocation.jsx lines 74-79 is now wired correctly and detectHeaders fires on every bankCsv/invCsv text change. Full wizard happy-path ran end-to-end without any manual Auto-detect click: name/period → bank-csv-sample → invoice-csv-sample → all 5 required map-* selects auto-populated within 900ms → Continue → Validate → Continue → landed on /allocations/{id} with 4 fully matched receipts (£3,700.00).
critical_code_review_comments: NewAllocation.jsx line 70: `loading` state is shared between detectHeaders (auto + manual), validate() and submit(). With the auto-debounced useEffect now running, a stray race could briefly disable the wizard-next button while a background detect is in-flight (the Step-3 Continue click failed once during retest until I added an extra 500ms wait). Consider using a dedicated `detecting` boolean for the silent path so wizard navigation never depends on auto-detect timing.

Emergent badge (#emergent-badge) intercepts pointer events on the bottom-right wizard-next button at viewport heights ≤900px. Playwright needed force=true or DOM hiding to click Continue on step 3/4. Real users on smaller laptops may experience the same — consider increasing the bottom padding of the wizard footer or repositioning the badge.
seed_data_creation: Created allocation run 'TEST_AutoDetect_v4' (id=a65b0f62-b820-4612-b244-9c6c4c9577de) during the end-to-end retest. Can be deleted from /allocations if cleanup is needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CRITICAL BUG FROM ITERATION 3 IS FIXED. The debounced useEffect at NewAllocation.jsx:74-79 watches [bankCsv, invCsv] and calls detectHeaders(true) after 400ms idle. Verified via Playwright: pasted SAMPLE_BANK then SAMPLE_INV via the sample buttons, waited 900ms, and inspected the 5 required selects — all populated correctly (bank_reference=Reference, bank_amount=Amount, invoice_number=InvoiceNumber, invoice_debtor=Debtor, invoice_amount=Amount). End-to-end allocation succeeded with 4 fully matched lines. NOTE for next runs: the emergent-badge overlay intercepts the wizard-next button at viewport height 900; use force=true or hide #emergent-badge via JS before clicking Continue on step 3/4.
rca of the issue: Iteration 3 RCA was correct — the useEffect was missing. Main agent re-applied the fix and it is now present at lines 74-79: `useEffect(() => { if (!bankCsv && !invCsv) return; const t = setTimeout(() => { detectHeaders(true); }, 400); return () => clearTimeout(t); }, [bankCsv, invCsv]);`. detectHeaders takes a silent flag (true) so it doesn't toast errors or flip the global `loading` state visibly during auto runs. Verified working end-to-end.
